summary: 23 bank-research items kept across 2 banks (JPM 18, Goldman 6) from 60 tweets considered over the last 4 days. JPMorgan Asset Management's mid-year 'Guide to the Markets' charts (relayed mostly by Mike Zaccardi, one by dailychartbook) dominate, covering equity valuations, S&P 500 AI/mega-cap concentration, and consumer macro data. Goldman items split between demographic/peak-consumer-population research and Dow/AI-adoption data points. One item (21% AI-adoption stat) crosses over, tagged to both JPM and Goldman. No @neilksethi / @neilksethinew tweets appeared in this collection window. No duplicate/relayed charts required dedup — every kept item is a distinct chart.

banks.theme: JPM — JPMorgan Asset Management (JPMAM) Guide-to-the-Markets charts, relayed by Mike Zaccardi and dailychartbook — equity valuation snapshot (P/E, CAPE, regional/sector multiples), S&P 500 AI/mega-cap concentration, GDP mix (AI capex vs. consumption), and consumer macro (gas prices, household debt, oil prices)

items.author: dailychartbook
items.text:
"Bringing the American Dream back into reach will be essential to powering the next 250 years of American prosperity"

-JPMorgan https://t.co/dbeu3sQe6b

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text:
US stocks are cheaper today than at the start of the year JPMAM

EM just an 11.7x P/E
How about Japan... near 17x https://t.co/f2Uy2CwBw7

items.text: Earnings growth dominates the YTD return contribution across regions JPMAM https://t.co/CFpMYcwkfr

items.text: Bond yields look much better today vs 15yr median interest rates JPMAM https://t.co/RDTiyCe7wh

items.text: Gas prices back to where they were in August-Sept 2023... keep in mind that incomes have risen since then too JPMAM https://t.co/OVZTU9YyCG

items.text: Household debt remains very low (vs disposable income) JPMAM https://t.co/tEkKi8P7AE

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: JPMAM AI adoption figure matches Goldman's AI tracker... 21% of firms using AI https://t.co/pJdRlrVjNM

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: AI investment (included in Business Fixed Investment) is a massive GDP contributor right now... Consumption rather puny JPMAM https://t.co/foz86Ac2YW

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: The S&P 500's 10% YTD gain and 9% drawdown halfway home is pretty calm... especially for a midterm year JPMAM https://t.co/de7fjRXKCi

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: Energy is the cheapest S&P 500 sector, and it has the highest dividend yield JPMAM https://t.co/fUlaICMoXi

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: Value vs Growth P/E spread... narrowest in 8 years JPMAM https://t.co/Y5nA4ZroDR

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: Median S&P 500 member P/E... 16.2x... certainly not a nosebleed level JPMAM https://t.co/czgvofTASf

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text:
AI hyperscalers are now 16.1% of the S&P 500
Semiconductors 19.2%

Both trade with a market multiple JPMAM

In all, 51.5% of the SPX is AI-related https://t.co/5R31o8O2f1

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: Mag 7 now with a somewhat modest P/E, but 38% EPS growth this year... Value 7? JPMAM https://t.co/yimFdeG3OV

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: $LLY $MU enter the S&P 500 top 10... the group tends to reshuffle quite often JPMAM https://t.co/oliBwJIJH0

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: S&P 500 biggest 10 now trades at just 21.6x P/E.. it was above 30x just a handful of months ago JPMAM https://t.co/Zi8rnNEr8V

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: SPX steady at 20.4x P/E ahead of #America250 JPMAM https://t.co/h5jsD48RGi

banks.theme: Goldman — Goldman Sachs demographics research (peak consumer population by country, US population dynamics) plus Dow index composition (GOOGL/CAT/GS weights) and Goldman's AI adoption tracker

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: GS: US - Average spending peaks ages 35-44 and declines sharply for 65+, the only growing share of US population https://t.co/k8FmNMH5ci

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: GS: The CBO expects deaths to exceed births starting in 2030 in the US and peak pop. in 2056 https://t.co/dgcDlIHLrP

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: GS: The developed world has likely reached peak consumer population aged 35-55, and will be significantly lower under zero migration / low fertility scenarios in the future https://t.co/zaYtipJbl7

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text: GS: Countries are at different points on the Demographic Wave for peak consumers (ages 35-55) with India pre peak, US early peak, China mid peak, and Japan post peak https://t.co/eYeBYT5JVW

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text:
$GOOGL is now 4.06% of the DJIA... 

$CAT & GS still almost one-quarter of the Dow https://t.co/Cxmdi5IWy1
